qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated terminal WebSocket exposes arbitrary shell access in edit mode
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same `/terminal/ws` endpoint in `marimo/_server/api/endpoints/terminal.py`, with `websocket.accept()` and PTY creation reachable without any `validate_auth()` check; the upstream fix adds that missing authentication gate before accepting the socket.

selected_finding.title: Unauthenticated terminal WebSocket exposes arbitrary shell access in edit mode

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The sole qualifying finding matches the advisory's root cause exactly and the judge rerun reproduced unauthorized terminal-shell command execution against a token-protected Marimo edit server.
evidence: At vulnerable commit `78489d4621dc4358dc5974af0c727feb0b7331cb`, `marimo/_server/api/endpoints/terminal.py` accepts `/terminal/ws` after only edit-mode and terminal-support checks; unlike the normal WebSocket validator path, it performs no `validate_auth()` call before `websocket.accept()` and `pty.fork()`.

Upstream fix commit `c24d4806398f30be6b12acd6c60d1d7c68cfd12a` inserts `if app_state.enable_auth and not validate_auth(websocket): ... return` into the same terminal endpoint, confirming the advisory-described missing-auth root cause.

The judge reran `bash reproduction/run.sh` from a copied workspace under `.judge/poc_runs/unauthenticated_terminal_websocket_rce/`; [run.log](/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_1913fc91/.judge/poc_runs/unauthenticated_terminal_websocket_rce/run.log) shows a `root@...:/app#` PTY prompt, the attacker-supplied `printf 'EVOHUNT_TERMINAL_PWNED\n'`, the emitted proof string, and `[evohunt] verified unauthenticated terminal access despite token-protected server`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC started a token-protected edit-mode Marimo server, connected to `/terminal/ws` without any access token or authenticated headers, reached a root PTY prompt, and printed `EVOHUNT_TERMINAL_PWNED` from attacker-supplied shell input.
